content_en:
The Humility in Prayer

THE SAYING OF ALLAH,

“Tt is the believers who are successful: those who are humble in their prayer...”

[Quran - AL-MU'IMINUN (23): 1-2]

APPENDIX I-III Ibn al-Qayyim on Khushi‘ Ibn al-Qayyim on Hypocritical Khushi‘ Ibn Taymiyyah on The Ruling of Khushi‘ in Prayer

Translated from the original Arabic by Aba Rumaysah

THE AUTHOR Abi’l-Faraj ibn Rajab

Khushi‘ The Meaning of Khushi‘ Hypocritical Humility The Source of Khushi‘ The first thing to be lost is khushi‘ Beneficial Knowledge The True Slaves of Allah

Actions of prayer conducive to Khushi‘

Being Attentive in Prayer

Supplication Loving the humble and poor Arrogance

Ibn al-Qayyim on Khushi‘

Ibn al-Qayyim on Hypocritical Khushi‘

Ibn Taymiyyah on The Ruling of Khushi‘ in Prayer

INDEX OF VERSES INDEX OF AHADITH INDEX OF SECTS

INDEX OF ARABIC WORDS

THE AUTHOR Hafiz Abi’l-Faraj ibn Rajab al-Hanbali

He is the Imam and Hafiz, Zaynu’l-Din ‘Abdur-Rahman ibn Ahmad ibn ‘Abdir-Rahman ibn al-Hasan ibn Muhammad ibn Abt- |-Barakat Mas‘id al-Sulami al-Hanbali al-Dimashqi. His agnomen was Abi’l-Faraj, and his nickname was Ibn Rajab, which was the nickname of his grandfather who was born in that month.

He was born in Baghdad in 736H and was raised by a knowledgeable and pious family. He died on a Monday night, the fourth of Ramadan, 795H in al-Humariyyah, Damascus.

He learned and took knowledge from the greatest scholars of his time. In Damascus, he studied under Ibn al-Qayyim, Zaynu'l-Din al-‘Iraqi, ibn an-Naqib, Muhammad ibn Isma‘il alKhabbaz, Dawid ibn Ibrahim al-‘Attar, ibn Qati al-Jabal and Ahmad ibn ‘Abdu-l-Hadi al-Hanbali. In Makkah, he heard from al-Fakhr ‘Uthman ibn Yasuf al-Nuwayri. In Jerusalem, he heard from alHafiz al-‘Ala’l. In Egypt, he heard from Sadru’l-Din Abi’l-Fath al-Maydimi and Nasiru’l-Din ibn al-Mulik.

Many students of knowledge came to him to study under him.

Hafiz Abi'l-Faraj ibn Rajab al-Hanbali

Amongst the most famous of his students were: Abi’l-‘Abbas Ahmad ibn Aba Bakr ibn ‘Ali al-Hanbali; Aba’l-Fadl Ahmad ibn Nasr ibn Ahmad; Dawid ibn Sulayman al-Mawsili; ‘Abdu’lRahman ibn Ahmad ibn Muhammad al-Mugri’; Zayni’l-Din ‘Abdu’l-Rahman ibn Sulayman ibn Abi’l-Karam; Abi Dharr alZarkashi; al-Qadi ‘Ala’t’l-Din ibn al-Laham al-Ba‘li; and Ahmad ibn Sayfa’l-Din al-Hamawi.

Ibn Rajab devoted himself to knowledge and spent the vast majority of his time researching, writing, authoring, teaching, and giving legal rulings.

Many scholars praised him for his vast knowledge, asceticism and expertise in the Hanbali school of thought. Ibn Qadi Shuhbah said of him, ‘He read and became proficient in the various fields of science. He engrossed himself with the issues of the madhab until he mastered it. He devoted himself to the occupation of knowledge of the texts, defects and meanings of the hadith.”

Ibn Hajr said of him, ‘He was highly proficient in the science of hadith in terms of the names of reporters, their biographies, their paths of narration and awareness of their meanings.”

Ibn Muflih said of him, ‘He is the Shaykh, the great scholar, the Hafiz, the ascetic, the Shaykh of the Hanbali madhab and he authored many beneficial works.”

‘Ibn Qadi al-Shuhbah, 7zrikA, vol. 3, p. 195. * ibn Hajr, Jnba’u'/-Ghamr, vol. 1, p. 460.

* al-Magqsad al-Arshad, vol. 2, p. 81.

He wrote many beneficial works, some of them outstanding such as a/-Qawa‘d al-Kubra_f'l-Furi‘ about which it was said, ‘It is one of the wonders of this age.”* His commentary to Al-Tirmidhi is said to be the most extensive and best ever written so much so that al-‘Iragi; about whom ibn Hajr said, ‘He was the wonder of his age’; would ask for his help when compiling his own commentary to the same book.

= Moreover he has many valuable monographs explaining various ahadith such as: Sharh Hadith Ma Dhi’bani Jai‘an Ursila fi Ghanam, Ikhtiyar al-Awla Sharh Hadith Ikhtisam al-Mala alAla, Nar al-Iqtibas fi Sharh Wastyyah al-Nabi i ibn ‘Abbas, and Kashfu'l-Kurbah fi Wasfi Hali Abli-l-Ghurbab.

= In exegesis his works include: Tafsir Surah al-Ikblas, Tafsir seerah al-Fatibaly, Tafsir seerah al-Nasr, and al-Istighna’ bi'lQuran.

= In hadith his works include: Sharh Wal al-Tirmidhi, Fathu’lBari Sharh Sabib al-Bukharé; and Jami‘ al-Ulim wa’l-Hikam.

= In biographies his works include the monumental Dhay/ ‘ala. Tabaqati'l-Hanabilab.

= In exhortation his works include: Lata’if al-Ma‘anf and al-Takhwif min al-Nar.

‘ibn ‘Abdu’l-Hadi, Dhay/ ‘ala Tabagat ibn Rajab, p. 38.

With the Name of Allah, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful It is His aid that we seek and “all praise is due to Allah, Lord of the Worlds.”

The Hafiz and ‘Allamah, Zayn al-Din ibn al-Shaykh Aba’l-‘Abbias Ahmad ibn Rajab, may Allah fill his life with blessings, said:

This is a treatise we have written concerning humility (Abushi’') and the hearts meekness and breaking (inkisar)' before the Lord.

All praise is due to Allah, mender of hearts which have broken for His sake and, by His grace, forgiver of the sins of the penitent. | testify that none has the right to be worshipped save Allah Alone; He has no partners and there is nothing like Him. I testify that Muhammad is His servant and Messenger; He sent him with

''The term ‘broken hear?’ refers to a heart that is filled with humility, subservience, and quietude as a result of its love and awe of Allah, Most High.

guidance and the Religion of Truth that it may reign supreme over all religions,> He gave him the choice of being a ProphetKing or a Messenger-Servant and he chose the station of servitude coupled with messengership.’

He (4) would say, “O Allah, make me live as one lowly and humble (miskin), make me die as one lowly and humble, and resurrect me amongst the ranks of the lowly and humble,’”* thereby alluding to the nobility and excellence of this station. Peace and blessings be upon him, his family, his Companions, and those who hold tightly to his rope after him.

In His Book, Allah, Glorious and Most High, has commended those who are meek (mukhbit) before Him, and those who have broken in the face of His grandeur; rendered submissive (Ahadi‘) and humble (&Aashi‘) in its presence. He, Most High, says,

On SOG Ci Cica ek;

* as Allah says, “It is He who has sent His Messenger with guidance and the Religion of Truth to exalt it over every other religion even though the polytheists detest it.” [a/-Tawbabh (9): 33] cf. a/-Fath (48): 28 and a/-Saff (61): 9

* The discussion on this will follow in the third chapter.

* Tirmidhi #2352 on the authority of Anas and he said it was gharib, ibn Majah #4126 on the authority of Abi Sa‘id, and Tabarani, a~Dx'‘a on the authority of ‘Ubadah ibn al-Samit, all with da‘if isnads, however the hadith is sahih.

It was also recorded on the authority of Abu Sa‘id by Hakim #7911 who said it was sahih with Dhahabi agrecing; it was also ruled sahih by Suyat, a/-/ami‘ alSaphir #1454, and Albani, Sabih al-Jami‘ #1261, al-Sabibah #308, al-Inva’ #861. cf. Sakhawi, Magasid al-Hasanah #166

“They outdid each other in good actions, calling out to Us in yearning and awe, and humbling themselves to Us.”*

ROA ON SOAIIISS) Sd Guah

Leeder te cae PATS PSEA | PEN | POOR BO | 1 Crh yor ally ue Ny ally

He describes the believers as those who have husha‘ in the best action of worship they perform, and perform diligently,

ORS PeIAUiOs jal “It is the believers who are successful: those who are humble in their prayer...”

” al-Mu'iminin (23): 1-2

He describes those who have been granted knowledge as people having khushi‘ when they hear His words rehearsed to them,

BIG ree oe ia Geet it

“Certainly, when it is recited to them, those who were given knowledge fall on their faces in prostration, saying, ‘Glory be to our Lord! The promise of our Lord is truly fulfilled!’ Weeping, they fall to the ground in prostration, and it increases them in humility.”*

The Meaning of Khushi‘

The basic meaning of &hushui‘ is the softness of the heart, its being gentle, still, submissive, broken, and yearning. The Messenger of Allah (4g) said, “Of a surety there is a morsel of flesh in the body, if it is sound, the whole body will be sound, but if it is corrupt, the whole body will be corrupt. It is the heart.”

When the heart is humble, so too is the hearing, seeing, head, and face; indeed all the limbs and their actions are humbled, even speech. This is why the Prophet (4) would say in his bowing

" Bukhari #52-2051 and Muslim #1599

The Meaning of Khushi‘

(mk), “My hearing, sight, bones, and marrow are humbled to You,” '’ another narration has, “and whatever my foot carries.

One of the Salaf saw a man fidgeting in his prayer and remarked, ‘If the heart of this person was humble, so too would his limbs be.’ This is reported from Hudhayfah (radiyAMlahu ‘anhu)'* and Sa‘id ibn al-Musayyib,” and it is also reported from the Prophet (4%) but is not authentic."

Mas‘adi reports on the authority of a person who narrated to him that ‘Ali ibn Abi Talib (radiyAliahu ‘anhu) said concerning His saying,

“,..those who are humble in their prayer...”

‘It refers to the humility of the heart, that you soften your shoulder for a Muslim (praying next to you), and that you do not

" Muslim #771 on the authority of ‘Ali.

"Ahmad #960 on the authority of ‘Ali and it was declared sahih by ibn Khuzaymah #607

" Tbn Nasr al-Marwazi, 7a‘zim Qadr al-Salah #150 with a da‘if isnad

'’ Marwazi #151 and ibn al-Mubarak, a/Zuwhd #419 with a da‘if isnad. "It was ruled mawdat by Albani, a/-Da'ifab #110 and a/-/nva’ #373

"* al-Mwiminin (23): 2

" Waki’, a/Zuhd #328, ibn al-Mubiarak, a/-Zuhd #1148 with a da‘if isnad. Suyiiti, a/-Durr a/-Manthir, vol. 10. pg. 557 records that ibn ‘Umar said in com-

look here and there in your prayer.”

‘Ata’ ibn al-Sa’ib reported on the authority of a person that ‘Ali (radiy Wahu ‘anhu) said, ‘Khusha‘ refers to the humility of the heart and that you do not look left and right.’

‘Aliibn Aba Talhah narrated that ibn ‘Abbas said in commentary to the above verse, ‘Those who are fearful and still.’

Ibn Shawdhab narrated that al-Hasan, may Allah have mercy upon him, said, ‘Humility had taken root in their hearts so their gazes were lowered and their limbs became subservient.’'*

Mansir reported that Mujahid said, “The foundation of &hushii‘ is in the heart and quietude in prayer.’
content_id:
Kekhusyukan dalam Salat

FIRMAN ALLAH,

"Sungguh beruntung orang-orang yang beriman: yaitu orang-orang yang khusyuk dalam salat mereka..."

[Quran - AL-MU'MINUN (23): 1-2]

LAMPIRAN I-III Ibn al-Qayyim tentang Khusyuk, Ibn al-Qayyim tentang Khusyuk yang Munafik, Ibn Taymiyyah tentang Hukum Khusyuk dalam Salat

Diterjemahkan dari bahasa Arab asli oleh Abu Rumaysah

PENULIS Abu'l-Faraj ibn Rajab

Khusyuk, Makna Khusyuk, Kekhusyukan yang Munafik, Sumber Khusyuk, Hal pertama yang hilang adalah khusyuk, Ilmu yang Bermanfaat, Hamba-hamba Allah yang Sejati

Amalan-amalan salat yang mendukung Khusyuk

Perhatian Penuh dalam Salat

Du'a, Mencintai orang-orang yang rendah hati dan fakir, Kesombongan

Ibn al-Qayyim tentang Khusyuk

Ibn al-Qayyim tentang Khusyuk yang Munafik

Ibn Taymiyyah tentang Hukum Khusyuk dalam Salat

INDEKS AYAT, INDEKS HADITH, INDEKS ALIRAN

INDEKS ISTILAH ARAB

PENULIS Hafiz Abu'l-Faraj ibn Rajab al-Hanbali

Beliau adalah al-Imam dan al-Hafiz, Zayn al-Din 'Abdur-Rahman ibn Ahmad ibn 'Abdir-Rahman ibn al-Hasan ibn Muhammad ibn Abu'l-Barakat Mas'ud al-Sulami al-Hanbali al-Dimasyqi. Kuniyah beliau adalah Abu'l-Faraj, dan julukan beliau adalah Ibn Rajab, yaitu julukan kakek beliau yang lahir pada bulan Rajab.

Beliau lahir di Baghdad pada tahun 736 H dan dibesarkan dalam keluarga yang berilmu dan saleh. Beliau wafat pada malam Senin, tanggal empat Ramadan 795 H di al-Humariyyah, Damaskus.

Beliau menimba ilmu dari para ulama terbesar pada zamannya. Di Damaskus, beliau belajar kepada Ibn al-Qayyim, Zayn al-Din al-'Iraqi, Ibn al-Naqib, Muhammad ibn Isma'il al-Khabbaz, Dawud ibn Ibrahim al-'Attar, Ibn Qadi al-Jabal, dan Ahmad ibn 'Abdul-Hadi al-Hanbali. Di Makkah, beliau mendengar dari al-Fakhr 'Uthman ibn Yusuf al-Nuwayri. Di Yerusalem, beliau mendengar dari al-Hafiz al-'Ala'i. Di Mesir, beliau mendengar dari Sadr al-Din Abu'l-Fath al-Maydumi dan Nasir al-Din ibn al-Mulik.

Banyak penuntut ilmu yang datang kepada beliau untuk belajar.

Hafiz Abu'l-Faraj ibn Rajab al-Hanbali

Di antara murid beliau yang paling terkenal adalah: Abu'l-'Abbas Ahmad ibn Abu Bakr ibn 'Ali al-Hanbali; Abu'l-Fadl Ahmad ibn Nasr ibn Ahmad; Dawud ibn Sulayman al-Mawsili; 'Abdur-Rahman ibn Ahmad ibn Muhammad al-Muqri'; Zayn al-Din 'Abdul-Rahman ibn Sulayman ibn Abu'l-Karam; Abu Dharr al-Zarkashi; al-Qadi 'Ala' al-Din ibn al-Laham al-Ba'li; dan Ahmad ibn Sayf al-Din al-Hamawi.

Ibn Rajab mencurahkan dirinya untuk ilmu dan menghabiskan sebagian besar waktunya untuk meneliti, menulis, mengarang, mengajar, dan memberikan fatwa.

Banyak ulama memuji beliau atas keluasan ilmunya, kezuhudannya, dan keahliannya dalam madhab Hanbali. Ibn Qadi Shuhbah berkata tentang beliau, 'Beliau membaca dan menjadi mahir dalam berbagai bidang ilmu. Beliau mendalami masalah-masalah madhab hingga menguasainya. Beliau mencurahkan diri untuk menguasai ilmu tentang teks-teks, 'ilal (cacat), dan makna-makna hadith.'

Ibn Hajr berkata tentang beliau, 'Beliau sangat mahir dalam ilmu hadith dari segi nama-nama perawi, biografi mereka, jalur-jalur periwayatan mereka, dan pemahaman terhadap makna-maknanya.'

Ibn Muflih berkata tentang beliau, 'Beliau adalah Syaikh, ulama besar, al-Hafiz, orang yang zuhud, Syaikh madhab Hanbali, dan beliau mengarang banyak karya yang bermanfaat.'

'Ibn Qadi al-Shuhbah, Tarikh, jil. 3, hlm. 195. * Ibn Hajr, Inba'u'l-Ghamr, jil. 1, hlm. 460.

* al-Maqsad al-Arshad, jil. 2, hlm. 81.

Beliau menulis banyak karya yang bermanfaat, beberapa di antaranya luar biasa seperti al-Qawa'id al-Kubra fi'l-Furu' yang tentangnya dikatakan, 'Ia adalah salah satu keajaiban zaman ini.'* Syarah beliau terhadap Al-Tirmidhi dikatakan sebagai yang paling luas dan terbaik yang pernah ditulis, hingga al-'Iraqi; yang tentangnya Ibn Hajr berkata, 'Beliau adalah keajaiban zamannya'; meminta bantuan beliau ketika menyusun syarah beliau sendiri terhadap kitab yang sama.

= Selain itu, beliau memiliki banyak risalah berharga yang menjelaskan berbagai hadith seperti: Sharh Hadith Ma Dhi'bani Ja'i'an Ursila fi Ghanam, Ikhtiyar al-Awla Sharh Hadith Ikhtisam al-Mala' al-A'la, Nur al-Iqtibas fi Sharh Wasiyyah al-Nabi li ibn 'Abbas, dan Kashf al-Kurbah fi Wasfi Hali Ahl al-Ghurbah.

= Dalam bidang tafsir, karya-karya beliau meliputi: Tafsir Surah al-Ikhlas, Tafsir Surah al-Fatihah, Tafsir Surah al-Nasr, dan al-Istighna' bi'l-Quran.

= Dalam bidang hadith, karya-karya beliau meliputi: Sharh 'Ilal al-Tirmidhi, Fath al-Bari Sharh Sahih al-Bukhari, dan Jami' al-'Ulum wa'l-Hikam.

= Dalam bidang biografi, karya-karya beliau meliputi kitab monumental Dhayl 'ala Tabaqat al-Hanabilah.

= Dalam bidang nasihat, karya-karya beliau meliputi: Lata'if al-Ma'arif dan al-Takhwif min al-Nar.

'Ibn 'Abdul-Hadi, Dhayl 'ala Tabaqat Ibn Rajab, hlm. 38.

Dengan Nama Allah, Yang Maha Pengasih, Yang Maha Penyayang. Kepada-Nya kami memohon pertolongan dan "segala puji bagi Allah, Rabb semesta alam."

Al-Hafiz dan 'Allamah, Zayn al-Din ibn al-Syaikh Abu'l-'Abbas Ahmad ibn Rajab, semoga Allah memberkahi hidupnya, berkata:

Ini adalah risalah yang kami tulis mengenai kekhusyukan (khusyu') dan kelembutan serta kepecahan (inkisar) hati di hadapan Rabb.

Segala puji bagi Allah, yang memperbaiki hati-hati yang pecah demi-Nya dan, dengan karunia-Nya, mengampuni dosa-dosa orang yang bertaubat. Aku bersaksi bahwa tidak ada yang berhak disembah selain Allah semata; tiada sekutu bagi-Nya dan tiada sesuatu pun yang menyerupai-Nya. Aku bersaksi bahwa Muhammad adalah hamba dan Rasul-Nya; Dia mengutusnya dengan

''Istilah 'hati yang pecah' merujuk pada hati yang dipenuhi kerendahan hati, ketundukan, dan ketenangan sebagai hasil dari kecintaan dan kekaguman kepada Allah, Yang Maha Tinggi.

petunjuk dan Agama yang Benar agar mengungguli semua agama.> Dia memberinya pilihan antara menjadi Nabi-Raja atau Rasul-Hamba, dan beliau memilih kedudukan kehambaan yang disertai kerasulan.'

Beliau (ﷺ) bersabda, "Ya Allah, jadikanlah aku hidup sebagai orang yang rendah hati dan miskin, matikanlah aku sebagai orang yang rendah hati dan miskin, dan bangkitkanlah aku di antara barisan orang-orang yang rendah hati dan miskin,"* dengan demikian mengisyaratkan kemuliaan dan keutamaan kedudukan ini. Semoga salawat dan salam tercurah atas beliau, keluarga beliau, para Sahabat beliau, dan mereka yang berpegang teguh pada tali beliau setelahnya.

Dalam Kitab-Nya, Allah, Yang Maha Mulia dan Maha Tinggi, memuji orang-orang yang tunduk (mukhbit) kepada-Nya, dan mereka yang pecah di hadapan keagungan-Nya; menjadi patuh (khadi') dan rendah hati (khashi') di hadapan-Nya. Dia, Yang Maha Tinggi, berfirman,

On SOG Ci Cica ek;

* sebagaimana Allah berfirman, "Dialah yang mengutus Rasul-Nya dengan petunjuk dan Agama yang Benar untuk dimenangkan atas semua agama, meskipun orang-orang musyrik membencinya." [al-Tawbah (9): 33] lih. al-Fath (48): 28 dan al-Saff (61): 9

* Pembahasan tentang ini akan menyusul di bab ketiga.

* Tirmidhi #2352 dari riwayat Anas dan beliau menyatakannya gharib, Ibn Majah #4126 dari riwayat Abu Sa'id, dan Tabarani, al-Du'a dari riwayat 'Ubadah ibn al-Samit, semuanya dengan sanad da'if, namun hadith ini sahih.

Hadith ini juga diriwayatkan dari Abu Sa'id oleh Hakim #7911 yang menyatakannya sahih dan al-Dhahabi menyetujuinya; juga dinilai sahih oleh al-Suyuti, al-Jami' al-Saghir #1454, dan al-Albani, Sahih al-Jami' #1261, al-Sahihah #308, al-Irwa' #861. lih. al-Sakhawi, Maqasid al-Hasanah #166

"Mereka berlomba-lomba dalam kebaikan, menyeru kepada Kami dengan penuh harap dan takut, dan mereka merendahkan diri kepada Kami."*

ROA ON SOAIIISS) Sd Guah

Leeder te cae PATS PSEA | PEN | POOR BO | 1 Crh yor ally ue Ny ally

Dia menyifati orang-orang beriman sebagai mereka yang memiliki khusyuk dalam ibadah terbaik yang mereka lakukan, dan mereka laksanakan dengan tekun,

ORS PeIAUiOs jal "Sungguh beruntung orang-orang yang beriman: yaitu orang-orang yang khusyuk dalam salat mereka..."

" al-Mu'minun (23): 1-2

Dia menyifati orang-orang yang diberi ilmu sebagai mereka yang memiliki khusyuk ketika mendengar firman-Nya dibacakan kepada mereka,

BIG ree oe ia Geet it

"Sesungguhnya, ketika dibacakan kepada mereka, orang-orang yang telah diberi ilmu tersungkur sujud, seraya berkata, 'Maha Suci Rabb kami! Sungguh janji Rabb kami pasti dipenuhi!' Sambil menangis, mereka tersungkur sujud, dan hal itu menambah kekhusyukan mereka."*

Makna Khusyuk

Makna dasar khusyuk adalah kelembutan hati, kehalusannya, ketenangannya, ketundukannya, kepecahannya, dan kerinduannya. Rasulullah (ﷺ) bersabda, "Sesungguhnya di dalam tubuh ada segumpal daging, jika ia baik maka baiklah seluruh tubuh, tetapi jika ia rusak maka rusaklah seluruh tubuh. Itulah hati."

Ketika hati khusyuk, maka pendengaran, penglihatan, kepala, dan wajah pun ikut khusyuk; bahkan seluruh anggota tubuh dan perbuatannya menjadi khusyuk, termasuk ucapan. Inilah sebabnya Nabi (ﷺ) bersabda dalam rukuknya

" Bukhari #52-2051 dan Muslim #1599

Makna Khusyuk

(ruku'), "Pendengaranku, penglihatanku, tulang-tulangku, dan sumsumku tunduk kepada-Mu," '' dalam riwayat lain disebutkan, "dan apa pun yang kakiku bawa.

Salah seorang Salaf melihat seseorang bergerak-gerak dalam salatnya dan berkomentar, 'Seandainya hati orang ini khusyuk, niscaya anggota tubuhnya pun akan khusyuk.' Ini diriwayatkan dari Hudhayfah (radhiyAllahu 'anhu)'* dan Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib," dan juga diriwayatkan dari Nabi (ﷺ) namun tidak sahih."

Mas'udi meriwayatkan dari seseorang yang menyampaikan kepadanya bahwa 'Ali ibn Abi Talib (radhiyAllahu 'anhu) berkata mengenai firman-Nya,

"...orang-orang yang khusyuk dalam salat mereka..."

'Yang dimaksud adalah kekhusyukan hati, bahwa engkau melunakkan bahumu untuk seorang Muslim (yang salat di sampingmu), dan bahwa engkau tidak

" Muslim #771 dari riwayat 'Ali.

"Ahmad #960 dari riwayat 'Ali dan dinyatakan sahih oleh Ibn Khuzaymah #607

" Ibn Nasr al-Marwazi, Ta'zim Qadr al-Salah #150 dengan sanad da'if

'' Marwazi #151 dan Ibn al-Mubarak, al-Zuhd #419 dengan sanad da'if. "Dinilai mawdu' oleh al-Albani, al-Da'ifah #110 dan al-Irwa' #373

"* al-Mu'minun (23): 2

" Waki', al-Zuhd #328, Ibn al-Mubarak, al-Zuhd #1148 dengan sanad da'if. Al-Suyuti, al-Durr al-Manthur, jil. 10. hlm. 557 mencatat bahwa Ibn 'Umar berkata dalam komen-

menoleh ke sana ke mari dalam salatmu."

'Ata' ibn al-Sa'ib meriwayatkan dari seseorang bahwa 'Ali (radhiyAllahu 'anhu) berkata, 'Khusyuk merujuk pada kerendahan hati dan bahwa engkau tidak menoleh ke kiri dan ke kanan.'

'Ali ibn Abu Talhah meriwayatkan bahwa Ibn 'Abbas berkata dalam tafsir ayat di atas, 'Mereka yang takut dan tenang.'

Ibn Shawdhab meriwayatkan bahwa al-Hasan, semoga Allah merahmatinya, berkata, 'Kekhusyukan telah mengakar dalam hati mereka sehingga pandangan mereka tertunduk dan anggota tubuh mereka menjadi patuh.''*

Mansur meriwayatkan bahwa Mujahid berkata, "Landasan khusyuk adalah di dalam hati dan ketenangan dalam salat.'
content_ar:
الخشوع في الصلاة هو روح العبادة وجوهرها، وبه يتمايز من يُقيم الصلاة حقاً عمن يُؤدّي صورتها الخارجية دون حضور قلبي. وقد بيّن علماء الإسلام أن الخشوع ينقسم إلى قسمين متلازمين: خشوع القلب بما يعنيه من حضور وإجلال وشعور بالوقوف بين يدي الله، وخشوع الجوارح بما يعنيه من سكون وتوقير وانصراف تام عما سوى الصلاة. وكلا القسمين يمد الآخر ويغذيه، فمتى استقر الخشوع في القلب انعكس أثره على الجوارح، ومتى انضبطت الجوارح أعانت القلب على الحضور.

ودلّت الآيات القرآنية الكريمة على مكانة الخشوع؛ إذ قال الله تعالى: «قَدْ أَفْلَحَ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ * الَّذِينَ هُمْ فِي صَلَاتِهِمْ خَاشِعُونَ». وفي الحديث الشريف أن النبي ﷺ كان يرى بعض الناس يعبثون في صلاتهم فقال: «لو خشع قلب هذا لخشعت جوارحه». ومن هنا نفهم أن السكون الظاهر الذي يصاحب الصلاة الخاشعة ليس غاية في ذاته، بل هو أثر لأمر باطن أسمى منه.

إن الخشوع روح الصلاة وجوهرها الحقيقي، وبه تُقبل الصلاة وتؤدي غرضها في تزكية النفس والارتقاء بها نحو الله تعالى. وقد جمع الشيخ ابن رجب الحنبلي رحمه الله في هذا الكتاب أسباب الخشوع ووسائل تحقيقه وموانعه، مستنيراً بالقرآن الكريم والسنة النبوية وأقوال السلف الصالح الذين كانوا يُمثّلون الخشوع في صلاتهم حتى كأنهم يرون الله تعالى.
